LAW AND MOTION 302, CROSS-DEFENDANTS ALBERT BOASBERG AND ARTHUR FONDEN'S MOTION TO SET ASIDE DEFAULT AND FOR LEAVE TO FILE A DEMURRER TO THE CROSS-COMPLAINT IS GRANTED. MOVING PARTIES HAVE SHOWN ENTITLEMENT TO THE RELIEF THEY SEEK PER THE MANDATORY RELIEF PROVISION OF CCP 473(B). WHILE CROSS-COMPLAINANTS ARE ENTITLED TO THEIR REASONABLY INCURRED FEES AND EXPENSES, THEY HAVE NOT SHOWN THAT ANY OF THEIR CLAIMED FEES AND EXPENSES WAS REASONABLY INCURRED. THE "RUSH TO THE COURTHOUSE" FILING OF A DEFAULT, WHEN IT WOULD BE APPARENT TO ANYONE WITH KNOWLEDGE OF THE PARTIES' DISPUTES THAT MR. BOASBERG AND MR. FONDEN INTENDED TO FULLY LITIGATE THE CROSS-COMPLAINT AGAINST THEM IS YET ANOTHER EXAMPLE OF THE ILL TREATMENT THAT THE PARTIES HAVE ACCORDED TO EACH OTHER. THE RECORD ON THIS MOTION SHOWS THAT CROSS-COMPLAINANTS TRIED TO USE AN ERROR MADE BY MR. BOASBERG TO OBTAIN A $40,000 PAYMENT. THAT CONDUCT SHOULD NOT BE REWARDED BY AN AWARD OF "REASONABLE" EXPENSES. THE PREVAILING PARTY IS TO PROVIDE A FORM OF ORDER THAT TRACKS THE TENTATIVE RULING VERBATIM AND SHOW COMPLIANCE WITH CRC 3.1312. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN. CLERK: ROSIE NOGUERA. REPORTER: SHERRY SAWYER, CSR #5976. (302/HK

(CACI 1501.) FOR THE PLEADING STAGE, ADEQUATE ALLEGATIONS HAVE BEEN MADE HERE, AS HAVE ALLEGATIONS OF SO-CALLED "MALICE." PRIOR COURT ORDERS MAY BE RELEVANT TO THE "PROBABLE CAUSE" ELEMENT, BUT ARE NOT NECESSARILY DISPOSITIVE. THE DEMURRER IS OVERRULED. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. A MOTION TO COMPEL FURTHER RESPONSES TO REQUESTS FOR ADMISSION, INTERROGATORIES, AND REQUESTS FOR PRODUCTION MUST BE ACCOMPANIED BY A SEPARATE STATEMENT. (CALIFORNIA RULES OF COURT, RULE 3.1345(A).) THE MOTION WAS NOT SUPPORTED BY A SEPARATE STATEMENT, WHICH IS GROUNDS TO DENY THE MOTION. (MILLS V. U.S. BANK (2008) 166 CAL.APP.4TH 871, 893.) THE COURT DECLINES TO CONSIDER GBC'S LATE-FILED SEPARATE STATEMENT, FILED AFTER FONDEN AND MANDELL OPPOSED THE MOTION TO COMPEL. GBC OFFERS NO JUSTIFICATION FOR THE LATE FILING. (CAL. RULES OF COURT, RULE 3.1300(D).) THE COURT DECLINES TO IMPOSE MONETARY SANCTIONS AGAINST GBC. (CODE CIV. PROC., 2023.010(H).) THE RECORD DOES NOT SHOW A LACK OF SUBSTANTIAL JUSTIFICATION FOR THE MOTION TO COMPEL AND THE COURT FINDS THE IMPOSITION OF SANCTIONS WOULD BE UNJUST UNDER THE CIRCUMSTANCES. (CODE CIV. PROC., 2023.030(A); BUNNELL V. SUPERIOR COURT OF ALAMEDA COUNTY (1967) 254 CAL.APP.2D 720, 724.). ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. CLERK K. LIU, NOT REPORTED. (D302/JPT)

Notice Of Motion To Compel Further Discovery Responses From Arthur O. Fonden And Scott J. Mandell
Off Calendar May-08-2019 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Atty. Robert Knox of Law Offices of Robert Knox 415-234-6913 appeared for plaintiff. / Atty. Ray K Shahani of Attorney at Law 650-348-1444 appeared for defendants.

Hearing held, after heard both sides argument. The Court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: DEFENDANTS GUERNEVILLE BUSINESS CORPORATION, INC., AND RAY SHAHANI'S Motion To Compel Further Discovery Responses From Arthur O. Fonden And Scott J. Mandell is DENIED. A motion to compel further responses to requests for admission, interrogatories, and requests for production must be accompanied by a separate statement. (California Rules of Court, rule 3.1345(a).) The motion was not supported by a separate statement, which is grounds to deny the motion. (Mills v. U.S. Bank (2008) 166 Cal.App.4th 871, 893.) The court declines to consider GBC's late-filed separate statement, filed after Fonden and Mandell opposed the motion to compel. GBC offers no justification for the late filing. (Cal. Rules of Court, rule 3.1300(d).) The court declines to impose monetary sanctions against GBC. (Code Civ. Proc., 2023.010(h).) The record does not show a lack of substantial justification for the motion to compel and the court finds the imposition of sanctions would be unjust under the circumstances. (Code Civ. Proc., 2023.030(a); Bunnell v. Superior Court of Alameda County (1967) 254 Cal.App.2d 720, 724.). Order signed in open court. Clerk K. Liu, not reported. (D302/JPT)

RULING - The Court adopted its tentative ruling. CROSS-DEFENDANTS ARTHUR FONDEN AND ALBERT BOASBERG'S DEMURRER TO CROSS COMPLAINT - The "probable cause" element of a "malicious prosecution" action is: "no reasonable person in defendant's circumstances would have believed that there were reasonable grounds to bring the lawsuit against plaintiff." (CACI 1501.) For the pleading stage, adequate allegations have been made here, as have allegations of so-called "malice." Prior court orders may be relevant to the "probable cause" element, but are not necessarily dispositive. The demurrer is OVERRULED. Order signed in open court. The Court confirmed its tentative ruling. Cross-defendants Albert Boasberg and Arthur Fonden's motion to set aside default and for leave to file a demurrer to the cross-complaint is granted. Moving parties have shown entitlement to the relief they seek per the mandatory relief provision of CCP 473(b). While cross-complainants are entitled to their reasonably incurred fees and expenses, they have not shown that any of their claimed fees and expenses was reasonably incurred. DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jun-15-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Attorney Albert Boasberg telephone number 650-755-6960 for Plaintiffs Scott Mandell and Arthur Fonden.
Attorney Ray Shahani telephone number for Defendant Guerneville Business Corporation, Inc.

The matter was argued. Upon conclusion of argument, the Court confirmed its tentative ruling. DEFENDANTS GUERNEVILLE BUSINESS CORPORATION and RAY SHAHANI'S DEMURRER TO FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT the malicious prosecution claim alleged by plaintiff Scott Mandell in the first amended complaint is sustained with twenty days leave to amend. The sole basis for the demurrer is that the dismissal of GBC's claims without prejudice against Mr. Mandell in the prior action was not a favorable termination for Mr. Mandell. Under some circumstances a dismissal without prejudice is a favorable termination for purposes of a malicious prosecution claim. However, Mr. Mandell has not sufficiently alleged that the dismissal of the claims against him constitutes a favorable termination because he has not alleged that the dismissal was made because of the lack of merit to GBC's claims and the corresponding lack of liability of Mr. Mandell. Mr. Mandell is granted leave to amend to allege that the dismissal constituted a favorable termination if he can do so in good faith. If Mr. Mandell is able to make such allegations, then the issue of whether the dismissal was a favorable termination would not be amenable to resolution on a demurrer. Order signed in open Court.

Judge: Harold Kahn. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR #5976. (302/HK)
